channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: R.E.M.’s Michael Stipe Has the Perfect Pick For Someone To Play Him in a Potential Biopic — Billie Eilish — ‘She’s Amazing’
item.description:
[INTRO MUSIC FADES OUT]
Host: Welcome to this week's music update. We're talking about the latest from Michael Stipe, the retired lead singer of R.E.M. He recently appeared on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, and had some interesting things to say.
[CLIP OF MICHAEL STIPE AND STEPHEN COLBERT TALKING]
Host: When asked about a potential R.E.M. biopic, Stipe suggested that Billie Eilish could play the young version of him in the movie. Colbert thought it was an interesting choice, noting that Eilish has similar eyes to Stipe.
[CLIP OF MICHAEL STIPE TALKING ABOUT BILLIE EILISH]
Host: Stipe also talked about meeting Billie Eilish briefly at a restaurant in Manhattan, where she held the door open for him. He was impressed by her politeness and sweetness.
[CLIP OF STEPHEN COLBERT OFFERING TO INTRODUCE STIPE TO EILISH]
Host: Stipe is currently working on his debut solo album, which is set to be released later this year. He gave Colbert a sneak peek into one of the songs, which features the sound of a tree hearing itself for the first time. Yes, you heard that right - a tree hearing itself for the first time.
[CLIP OF MICHAEL STIPE EXPLAINING THE SONG]
Host: Stipe also performed a new song on the show, called "The Rest of Ever". It's a beautiful, smoldering ballad that showcases his unique vocals.
[CLIP OF MICHAEL STIPE PERFORMING "THE REST OF EVER"]
Host: That's all for today's update. Michael Stipe's debut solo album is coming out later this year, and we can't wait to hear more from him.
[OUTRO MUSIC FADES IN]
